findings.title: Read-only tool requests dangerous parameters
findings.detail: Tool "http_error_explain" appears informational but accepts parameters (code) that enable code/file/secret access.

description: Resolve a Basename (.base.eth) to a wallet address on Base. Costs $0.001.

labels.name: x402_audit
labels.description: [x402] WORKFLOW: full scored audit of an x402 SELLER endpoint (a URL) in one call - verifies the 402 challenge, payment fields, Bazaar discovery metadata, and the origin's discovery surfaces; returns a 0-100 score, checks with evidence, and recommended fixes. Costs $0.01. Use this to grade an endpoi

labels.name: payment_proof
labels.description: [x402] Verify an x402 settlement from a tx hash: confirm the Base tx succeeded, decode its USDC transfers, optionally assert payTo/amount - returns a single verdict. Costs $0.003. Choose this when you only need the yes/no did-it-land answer; use settlement_assurance for the full scored report (proof

labels.name: settlement_assurance
labels.description: [x402] FLAGSHIP: from a tx hash, prove a completed x402 payment is real and sound - onchain success, USDC transfer match to an expected wallet/amount, a receipt, and an optional audit of the issuing endpoint - as one 0-100 scored report with a verdict. Costs $0.03. This is the most complete post-pay

labels.name: x402_discover
labels.description: [x402] Search the Coinbase x402 Bazaar for paid resources by natural-language query. Costs $0.001.

labels.name: x402_merchant
labels.description: [x402] List all x402 Bazaar resources under a merchant payTo wallet. Costs $0.001.

labels.name: usdc_normalize
labels.description: Convert USDC amounts between atomic units and human decimals. Costs $0.001.

labels.name: base_token_info
labels.description: ERC-20 name, symbol, decimals, total supply for a token on Base. Costs $0.001.

labels.name: base_allowance
labels.description: Check an ERC-20 spending allowance on Base; flags unlimited approvals. Costs $0.001.

labels.name: base_contract
labels.description: Determine whether a Base address is a contract or an EOA. Costs $0.001.

labels.name: base_usdc_transfers
labels.description: Recent USDC transfers (in and out) for an address on Base. Costs $0.002.

labels.name: jwt_decode
labels.description: [Dev] Decode a JWT header and payload (signature NOT verified). Costs $0.001.

labels.name: base64
labels.description: [Dev] Base64 encode or decode a string. Costs $0.001.

labels.name: text_stats
labels.description: [Dev] Word/sentence counts, reading time, and readability score for text. Costs $0.001.

labels.name: text_diff
labels.description: [Dev] Compact line-level diff between two texts. Costs $0.001.

labels.name: x402_test_vector
labels.description: [x402] Free: example x402 payment-requirement objects with field explanations.

labels.name: current_time
labels.description: FREE (no wallet needed): current time as ISO, unix, UTC, and optional local time for an IANA timezone.

labels.name: generate_uuid
labels.description: FREE (no wallet needed): generate UUID v4 values, up to 100 at once.

labels.name: hash_string
labels.description: FREE (no wallet needed): SHA-1/256/384/512 hex digest of a string.

labels.name: http_errors_list
labels.description: FREE (no wallet needed): index of documented HTTP status codes (use http_error_explain for a paid detailed explanation).

labels.name: quartermaster_info
labels.description: Free: catalog, prices, and payment instructions for this server.

findings.title: Read-only tool requests dangerous parameters
findings.detail: Tool "http_error_explain" appears informational but accepts parameters (code) that enable code/file/secret access.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: page_meta, summarize_url, settlement_assurance · Private data: dns_lookup, tx_status, wallet_balance, token_price, payment_receipt, resolve_basename, settlement_assurance, x402_merchant, base_token_info, current_time, generate_uuid, hash_string, http_errors_list · Public sink: settlement_assurance · Destructive: qr_generate.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: page_meta, summarize_url, settlement_assurance · Private data: dns_lookup, tx_status, wallet_balance, token_price, payment_receipt, resolve_basename, settlement_assurance, x402_merchant, base_token_info, current_time, generate_uuid, hash_string, http_errors_list · Public sink: settlement_assurance · Destructive: qr_generate

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "tx_decode" references sensitive artifacts (authorization). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: [Crypto/Base] Decode a Base transaction's calldata into the function it called and its arguments — ERC-20 transfer/approve and the EIP-3009 transferWithAuthoriz…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: page_meta, summarize_url, http_check, settlement_assurance · Private data: dns_lookup, tx_status, wallet_balance, x402_simulate, token_price, payment_receipt, resolve_basename, settlement_assurance, x402_merchant, base_token_info, current_time, generate_uuid, hash_string, http_errors_list · Public sink: settlement_assurance · Destructive: qr_generate.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: page_meta, summarize_url, http_check, settlement_assurance · Private data: dns_lookup, tx_status, wallet_balance, x402_simulate, token_price, payment_receipt, resolve_basename, settlement_assurance, x402_merchant, base_token_info, current_time, generate_uuid, hash_string, http_errors_list · Public sink: settlement_assurance · Destructive: qr_generate

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
